sandbox.allow-forms: Allows the page to submit forms. If this keyword is not used, form will be displayed as normal, but submitting it will not trigger input validation, sending data to a web server or closing a dialog.
sandbox.allow-modals: Allows the page to open modal windows (e.g., window.alert(), window.confirm(), window.print(), and <dialog> elements).
sandbox.allow-orientation-lock: Allows the page to lock the screen orientation.
sandbox.allow-pointer-lock: Allows the page to use the Pointer Lock API.
sandbox.allow-popups: Allows popups (like window.open(), target="_blank", and showModalDialog()). If this keyword is not used, the popup will silently fail to open.
sandbox.allow-popups-to-escape-sandbox: Allows a sandboxed document to open new windows without forcing the sandboxing flags upon them.
sandbox.allow-presentation: Allows embedders to have control over whether an iframe can start a presentation session.
sandbox.allow-same-origin: If this token is not used, the resource is treated as being from a special origin that always fails the same-origin policy (potentially preventing access to data storage/cookies and some JavaScript APIs).
sandbox.allow-scripts: Allows the page to run scripts (but not create popups). If this keyword is not used, this operation is not allowed.
sandbox.allow-top-navigation: Allows the resource to navigate the top-level browsing context (the one named _top).
sandbox.allow-top-navigation-by-user-activation: Allows the resource to navigate the top-level browsing context, but only if initiated by a user gesture.
sandbox.allow-top-navigation-to-custom-protocols: Allows navigations to non-http protocols built into browser or registered by a web site.
